method: post-hoc Python analysis on existing Phase 5b traces (no new C++ runs)

marker_channels.notes: Most robust marker — saturates at ~-226 across L36-L66 on token 0
Opposite polarity to #4386 at same layer (L42)
Similar magnitude to #2305 at adjacent layer

background_riders.notes: Appears in top-K alongside #4386 but does not saturate itself
Peak at L30 (between #2305 and #2232 ranges) but no positional pattern
Small negative peaks scattered across token positions

key_findings: 4 of 7 channels tested (markers #4386, #2232, #506, #2305) saturate 100% on token_index=0
#4386 is the largest-magnitude marker (~229 vs next largest #2232 ~84)
#4386 and #2232 saturate at the SAME layer (L42) with OPPOSITE polarity
#506 and #2305 are 6 layers apart with nearly identical saturation magnitudes (~37)
Background rider channels peak at <50% of smallest marker magnitude
Token-0 marker role is distributed across depths — no single channel covers full stack
